questions.text: If in a transistor both collector-base and base-emitter junctions are reversed biased then it is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In a typical operation one junction (the base-emitter junction) is forward biased while the other (the base-collector junction) is reverse biased.However, if both the collector-base and base-emitter junctions are reverse biased, the transistor is in the cutoff state. In this state, both junctions are blocking the majority carrier current flow, which means there is minimal current flowing through the transistor.
Incorrect as per given condition and fact.
Incorrect as per given condition and fact.
Incorrect as per given condition and fact.

questions.explanation: In a typical operation one junction (the base-emitter junction) is forward biased while the other (the base-collector junction) is reverse biased.However, if both the collector-base and base-emitter junctions are reverse biased, the transistor is in the cutoff state. In this state, both junctions are blocking the majority carrier current flow, which means there is minimal current flowing through the transistor.

questions.text: By reducing the absolute temperature of a black body to half, the total energy radiated will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per given condition and formula: E=σT4
Incorrect as per given condition and formula: E=σT4
Incorrect as per given condition and formula: E=σT4

The total energy radiated by a black body is governed by the Stefan-Boltzmann law. Mathematically, it’s expressed as: E=σT4where:E is the energy radiated per unit surface area,T is the absolute temperature of the body,and σ is the Stefan-Boltzmann constant. E'=σ(T/2)4 =σ(T4/16) =(1/16) σT4 E'=1/16ESo, if the absolute temperature of a black body is reduced to half, the total energy radiated will be reduced to 1/16th of its original value.

questions.explanation: The total energy radiated by a black body is governed by the Stefan-Boltzmann law. Mathematically, it’s expressed as: E=σT4where:E is the energy radiated per unit surface area,T is the absolute temperature of the body,and σ is the Stefan-Boltzmann constant. E'=σ(T/2)4 =σ(T4/16) =(1/16) σT4 E'=1/16ESo, if the absolute temperature of a black body is reduced to half, the total energy radiated will be reduced to 1/16th of its original value.

questions.text: Electric field at a distance of 20cm from a 4µC charge is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per given condition and formula:E=kq​/r2
Incorrect as per given condition and formula:E=kq​/r2

The electric field E due to a point charge q at a distance r from the charge is given by Coulomb’s law: E=kq​/r2where:k is Coulomb’s constant = 8.99×109Nm2/C2q is the charger is the distance from the charge.Substituting the given values (q = 4µC = 4x10−6C) & (r = 20cm = 0.2m) into the equation, we get:E=(8.99×109Nm2/C2)×(4×10−6C​)/(0.2m)2 =(8.99×109Nm2/C2)×(4×10−6C​)/(0.04m2 or 4×10-2m2)=8.99×109×10-6×102 N/C =8.99×105N/C≈9x105 N / C
Incorrect as per given condition and formula:E=kq​/r2

questions.explanation: The electric field E due to a point charge q at a distance r from the charge is given by Coulomb’s law: E=kq​/r2where:k is Coulomb’s constant = 8.99×109Nm2/C2q is the charger is the distance from the charge.Substituting the given values (q = 4µC = 4x10−6C) & (r = 20cm = 0.2m) into the equation, we get:E=(8.99×109Nm2/C2)×(4×10−6C​)/(0.2m)2 =(8.99×109Nm2/C2)×(4×10−6C​)/(0.04m2 or 4×10-2m2)=8.99×109×10-6×102 N/C =8.99×105N/C≈9x105 N / C

questions.text: If the peak value of alternating current is 7√2A then the mean square value of current will be?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The mean square value (or RMS value) of an alternating current (AC) is given by the peak value divided by the square root of 2.Given that the peak value of the AC is 7√2 Irms​=​Ipeak/√2​​ =7√2/√2 ​​​=7 ASo, the mean square (RMS) value of the current is 7 A.
Incorrect as per given condition: Irms​=​Ipeak/√2

questions.text: _is NOT a ferromagnetic material.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It is a ferromagnetic material.
Wood is not a ferromagnetic material.
It is a ferromagnetic material.
It is a ferromagnetic material.

questions.explanation: Nickel, cobalt, and iron are ferromagnetic materials, meaning they can be magnetized or attracted to a magnet. Wood, on the other hand, does not have this property. Therefore, wood is not a ferromagnetic material.
